Curriculum Digests Newsletter

Chapter 5 Evaluation

Winning the Technical Deep Dive

⏱️ 60 minutes

🎯 Learning Objectives

  • Understand how technical buyers use AI agents to evaluate vendors
  • Structure documentation for agent-assisted research
  • Prepare demo content that answers AI-generated questions
  • Create POC materials that close capability gaps

The AI-Assisted Champion

Your internal champion used to spend weeks gathering requirements, reading documentation, and building internal proposals. Now they have an AI assistant that can process your entire documentation set in minutes and surface answers to any technical question instantly.

This is the AI-assisted champion — a technical buyer armed with unprecedented research velocity. They're asking deeper questions earlier, validating claims faster, and building more comprehensive business cases than ever before.

Key Insight

Your champion's AI assistant is now your first technical evaluator. If it can't find clear answers in your documentation, you're creating friction in the deal.

How Technical Buyers Use AI Agents

📋 Requirements Mapping

"Does this platform support X? What about Y? List all integration capabilities." Champions use AI to systematically check requirements against your documentation.

🔍 Deep Technical Queries

"Explain how SAP Datasphere handles real-time data synchronization." Specific architectural questions that require detailed technical content to answer.

📊 Comparison Analysis

"Compare the security models of Vendor A vs Vendor B." AI synthesizes technical details from multiple sources to build comparison matrices.

📝 Proposal Building

"Draft an internal proposal for implementing this platform." Champions use AI to create business cases from your marketing and technical content.

The Speed Advantage

Technical evaluation that once took 4-6 weeks now happens in days. Your champion can:

  • Ingest your entire documentation library overnight
  • Cross-reference technical claims against third-party sources
  • Generate detailed questions for your technical team
  • Build comparison matrices across multiple vendors simultaneously
  • Draft implementation proposals before the first technical call

Documentation for Agents

Your documentation is no longer just for human readers — it's a primary data source for AI agents conducting technical evaluation. This requires a fundamental rethink of how you structure and present technical content.

The Three Principles of Agent-Ready Documentation

🏗️ Structured

Clear hierarchies, consistent formatting, explicit headers. AI agents rely on structure to understand content relationships and find specific information.

🔎 Queryable

Every technical claim should be answerable with a direct excerpt. If someone asks "Does X support Y?", the answer should be explicitly stated.

📚 Comprehensive

Gaps in documentation become gaps in AI responses. If a capability isn't documented, AI assumes it doesn't exist or downgrades confidence in your product.

Documentation Before

  • Written for linear reading
  • Marketing language mixed with technical
  • Capabilities implied, not stated
  • Scattered across PDFs and web pages

Documentation Now

  • Written for non-linear querying
  • Technical precision throughout
  • Explicit capability statements
  • Unified, crawlable documentation site

Documentation Checklist

  • Integration matrix — Complete list of supported integrations with explicit yes/no capability markers
  • Architecture diagrams — With text descriptions that AI can parse (diagrams alone aren't enough)
  • API reference — Complete endpoint documentation with examples
  • Security documentation — Compliance certifications, encryption specs, access controls
  • Performance benchmarks — Quantified performance claims with methodology
  • Deployment options — Clear documentation of cloud, on-prem, hybrid capabilities
Every technical question your champion might ask should have an explicit answer in your documentation.

Demo Strategy

Before the demo call, your champion's AI has already prepared a list of questions based on gaps it identified in your documentation. The demo is now a validation exercise, not a discovery session.

What AI Will Ask About Your Product

  • Edge cases — "How does the platform handle [unusual scenario]?"
  • Scalability limits — "What are the documented limits? What happens when you hit them?"
  • Integration specifics — "Show me the actual integration with [their system]"
  • Security in practice — "Walk me through how [security feature] actually works"
  • Failure modes — "What happens when [component] fails?"

The Demo Shift

Before: "Let me show you our key features."

After: "I see your AI identified these 5 technical questions. Let me address each one directly."

Preparing for AI-Generated Questions

🎯 Pre-Demo Query Simulation

Before major demos, feed your documentation to AI and ask it to generate technical questions. These are the questions your champion's AI is generating.

📝 Gap Documentation

For any question AI can't answer from your docs, create explicit documentation. The gap exists for every buyer using AI.

🔬 Deep Dive Content

Create detailed technical content for common AI-generated questions. If AI asks frequently, make the answer comprehensive.

📊 Validation Assets

Screenshots, videos, and recorded demos that prove capabilities. AI will tell champions to "verify during demo" — be ready.

POC Content

Proof of concept is where deals are won or lost. The AI-assisted champion enters POC with a detailed checklist of capability gaps to validate. Your POC content strategy must anticipate and close these gaps.

The Capability Gap Framework

Every technical requirement falls into one of four categories:

  • Verified — AI found explicit documentation confirming capability
  • Probable — AI inferred capability from related documentation
  • Uncertain — AI couldn't find clear evidence either way
  • Gap — AI found evidence suggesting limitation

Your POC content must move requirements from Uncertain → Verified and reframe Gaps with alternative approaches.

POC Content Assets

✅ Capability Validation Guides

Step-by-step guides that champions can follow to verify specific capabilities. "To verify real-time sync, follow these steps..."

📐 Reference Architectures

Pre-built architectures for common use cases that demonstrate best practices and full capability utilization.

🧪 Test Scripts

Documented test scenarios with expected outcomes. Makes POC validation systematic and defensible.

📋 Success Criteria Templates

Pre-defined success metrics that align with your strengths. Help champions structure POC evaluation in your favor.

POC Intelligence

If your champion's AI generates a question you can't answer, that's intelligence. Document it, create content for it, and close the gap for future deals.

🎯 Interactive Exercise: Technical Query Simulation

Stress test your technical documentation with AI:

  1. Gather your technical documentation — Product docs, API reference, architecture guides
  2. Feed to ChatGPT or Claude:
    • "Based on this documentation, what technical questions can't you answer?"
    • "If you were evaluating this product for enterprise deployment, what gaps would you identify?"
    • "Generate 10 tough technical questions a buyer might ask"
  3. Categorize the results:
    • Questions with clear answers in docs ✅
    • Questions where docs are vague ⚠️
    • Questions with no documented answer ❌
  4. Create a gap remediation plan — Prioritize by frequency and deal impact
  5. Draft new documentation for the top 3 gaps

Time: ~25 minutes

Key Takeaways

  • Technical buyers now use AI to accelerate evaluation from weeks to days
  • Documentation must be structured, queryable, and comprehensive for agent consumption
  • Demos have shifted from discovery to validation — AI prepares questions in advance
  • POC content should close capability gaps identified by AI research
  • Every unanswered AI question is an opportunity to improve documentation
